to be shorter than scheduled). Linux login servers login.eecs/login-mfa.eecs will also be patched and rebooted around this time. [Read more…] about January IRIS Server Patching As of about 11:51 AM: wireless networks are offline due to a power issue

wireless networks are offline due to a power issue in one of the campus network core sites. Wired networks are unaffected and continue to operate normally. We will update again when we have more information about the status of the campus network core

mation about the status of the campus network core. Update 13:36: Campus has begun restoring power to the network core using temporary connections, so we expect to see some wi-fi service resume. However, wi-fi may experience some continuing instabili

er, wi-fi may experience some continuing instability as the work to restore full power goes on. Update 17:02: Power has been restored and network services should be fully available. There may be some late-night wi-fi disruptions as staff perform some

late-night wi-fi disruptions as staff perform some cleanup work. Please contact us at help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any questions. Due to the Winter Holiday, Energy Curtailment, and New Year’s Holidays, the Berkeley campus is officially closed f

lidays, the Berkeley campus is officially closed for the period Wednesday, December 24 to Thursday, January 1, reopening on Friday, January 2, 2026. Additionally, much of campus is on reduced support for December 22-23, January 2, and the week of Jan

for December 22-23, January 2, and the week of January 5-9. All departmental computing servers will remain on and should be available during the break, but there will be no staff on site to provide any computing support or handle any requests. Automa

y computing support or handle any requests. Automatic monitoring systems will alert staff to critical infrastructure problems, and any such problems will be dealt with on a best-effort basis. The IRIS Helpdesk office will be closed Monday, December 2

Helpdesk office will be closed Monday, December 22 through Friday, January 2, re-opening fully on Monday, January 12. If you need assistance during the week of January 5-9, please email help@eecs, and we should be able to reply and schedule an appoi

d we should be able to reply and schedule an appointment with you if needed. Problem reports can be left on the Helpdesk voice mail (510-642-7777) or emailed to help@eecs during the campus closure period, but staff may not receive or reply to these m

iod, but staff may not receive or reply to these messages until at least January 5. Requests for network support or new IRIS accounts that are received after noon Thursday, December 18 may not be processed until January 5, 2026. During the break, Ins

essed until January 5, 2026. During the break, Instructional labs will be locked, but the main servers will remain online (for details, see the Instructional website ). Instructional support staff will occasionally monitor inst@eecs email over the br

l occasionally monitor inst@eecs email over the break. Please be aware, though, that staff may not be able to respond or reply to messages until at least January 5. Computing support staff will be monitoring their email when possible, so please use s

itoring their email when possible, so please use standard email addresses to report any urgent problems. As a reminder: Instructional Computing Environment: inst@eecs Research / Staff Computing Environment: help@eecs Information regarding campus IT c

nment: help@eecs Information regarding campus IT closures is available here: https://technology.berkeley.edu/news/campus-tech-support-during-winter-holidays Happy Holidays! A recent report of a discrepancy in our file server NFS exports helped us to

pancy in our file server NFS exports helped us to discover an error in our NFS export policies. The error would allow shares with the default NFS export setting to be mounted on some systems within EECS networks that they were not intended to be expo

CS networks that they were not intended to be exported to. We have corrected the error, and audited our NFS export policies. If you discover any unexpected change to availability of your home directory or project space, please feel free to reach out

y or project space, please feel free to reach out to help@eecs. Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, December 9th, 2025. Various IRIS Windows and Linux servers will be patched and rebooted Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday. PR

re scheduling a NetApp ONTAP upgrade, to apply a necessary update to our NetApp fileservers, from 8am to 12pm on Friday, November 28, 2025 . All Home directories and Project shares (CIFS/SMB and NFS) may experience disconnection during the update tha

may experience disconnection during the update that may cause data loss on active, open files. Please close your open files and save them before Friday morning. Services (e.g. webservice) that depend on this storage will also be temporarily down or

on this storage will also be temporarily down or unresponsive. When maintenance is completed, NFS clients should automatically resume normal operation, but a reboot should help if they don’t. CIFS clients (such as Windows and MacOS) may need to remo

ients (such as Windows and MacOS) may need to remount filesystems, or simply be rebooted. [Read more…] about NetApp Fileserver Maintenance, Friday November 28, 2025 Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, November 11th, 2025. V
